rationale: The raw LEA/TEST sequence checks the embedded this+0x0c address, not a nullable field value; the corrected comment removes the false null-gate claim while preserving the proven callback and RET 0x4 boundary.
The entry is a single JMP to CCockpit__dtor_base and is called by CDXCockpit__scalar_deleting_dtor, so the thunk name/comment are more accurate than destination-expanded decompiler text.
The body and sole CBattleEngine__Move caller match the source-aligned HandleLocks phases; plain RET/ECX preserve the prototype shape, so the signature change is rendering-only.
RET 0x4 proves one readerId argument, and raw stores establish the cooldown timestamps; the duplicate cleanup calls CDXMemoryManager__Free rather than the previously claimed OID helper.
The vtable slot, BattleEngine part accesses, HandleLocks call, actor movement, and aim/sound/effect phases match CBattleEngine__Move; the signature edit changes owner/parameter rendering only.
The raw free sequence uses offset 0x64; spelling the value as decimal-looking +100 is ambiguous in an otherwise hexadecimal list, so the corrected offset wording is exact.
The caller loads BattleEngine+0x57c and the body matches JetPart movement/effect ordering, supporting CBattleEngineJetPart__Move without changing the underlying prototype.
The sole JetPart Move caller and terrain/water sampling, near-ground acceleration, damping, pitch, and roll work match HandleGroundEffect; signature shape remains unchanged.
The JetPart receiver, height bands, velocity query, and x87 tiered return match GetFriction, while the float-return/ECX/no-stack-argument structure is unchanged.
The predicate tests the two special-air-move fields and is called from BattleEngine morph handling, matching GetIsDoingSpecialAirMove with no prototype-structure change.
Ground, squared-velocity, energy, and local-state gates match JetPart AutoLevel; the inherited CMonitor identity is stale and the prototype shape is preserved.
The WalkerPart caller and old/current walk-cycle copy, projected velocity, dominant-axis selection, advance, and pi wrap match UpdateWalkCycle; signature changes are rendering-only.
AND 0x20000 followed by CMP 1 makes the side-compatibility branch unreachable; effective flow reaches CCollisionSeekingRound__CheckCollisionFlags, exactly as the corrected bounded name/comment state.
RET 0x4 proves one stack input, which is forwarded through vslot +0x1ac and then to the score accumulator; the correction preserves the separate stale-prototype debt.
Raw instructions push ana_val, button, and controller before CGame__ReceiveButtonAction; the old comment's claim that ana_val is not passed is a callee-prototype artifact.
The function allocates and registers a 0x4c no-vtable definition record, structurally distinct from the runtime CWeapon object; WeaponDefinition is the safer bounded owner.
The 0x50 no-vtable registry record is a definition and not the larger vtable-backed runtime CExplosion; the corrected generic definition name avoids that conflation.
Null-guarded string replacements, an explicit destination zero, unattached allocations, append-without-clear behavior, and a seven-dword copy contradict the inherited blanket deep-copy/rebuild claim.
The null source path performs no write; the non-null path frees, zeroes, allocates strlen+1, stores, and copies the terminator, matching the proposed correction exactly.
Raw code performs the previously omitted +0x160 probe after vslot +0x24, although its result is unused before fallback; the corrected comment reflects the listing without overstating semantics.
The entry is exactly a JMP to CFlexArray__Free and has direct shutdown/unwind callers, so the inherited expanded-body/non-thunk interpretation is wrong.
The RTTI-resolved CFenrirMainGunAI vtable slot and full scan body support the bounded owner, and raw code contains the action-helper call denied by the current comment.
The entry is a single JMP to CTweak__dtor_base and is reached by static cleanup callers; the decompiler-expanded teardown belongs to the target, not this thunk entry.
The CGame loop callers and full update phases support CGame__Update, while ECX plus plain RET establish no explicit stack arguments; the bounded static/source-alignment comment is accurate.
RET 0x28 proves ten stack dwords while the saved prototype renders nine; raw loop bounds identify the extra argument and support the corrected RGB565 blit description and unresolved ABI debt.
Centre/radius distance, speed-like vtable samples, event-2000 dispatch, limit warnings, saved-event handling, and collision-scan callers support the corrected collision-event account.

Coordinate tests implement the documented 3x3-neighborhood exit rule and the raw call targets DispatchFilteredCollisionPair, not an exit callback; lower-level filtering and RET 0x8 corroborate the correction.
The 20-instruction body forwards ECX and two explicit inputs, computes length, skips only empty text, strips trailing LF but not CR, and RETs 0x8; the old wording mishandles LF-only input.
Both the global-mip bypass and failed-lock path reach the unconditional vtable+0x50 tail; only a successful lock copies RGB565 data, matching the corrected bounded upload comment.
Both exits RET 0x10, proving four stack dwords; only the first three are consumed before the pointed state is cleared, so the corrected comment accurately retains one unidentified argument.
RET 0x0c proves three stack dwords and raw stack accounting identifies the third as shadow_only_flag while the second is unused; height sampling and trace callers support the rest.
The allocation call passes only the outer CMech receiver before constructing and storing the guide, so no extra constructor argument exists at this callsite.
Only the button is read while RET 0x0c proves three dwords; adjustment attempts set handled even when clamped, so +0x30 gates notification after attempts rather than only successful changes.
The body reads one gate byte and RETs 0x4, disproving a second unused-context argument; the list walk and vslot dispatch conditions match the corrected comment.
RET 0x0c and stable-frame accounting prove three explicit inputs, not the published four; expression evaluation, fading, and the three packing modes support the corrected description.
The entry is one JMP to the MenuItem destructor target and is called by the mouse-sensitivity deleting destructor; destination-expanded decompile text must not be attributed to the thunk.
Raw code directly calls CD3DApplication__Reset3DEnvironment with two literal flags before the save path, so the former FUN label is that reset and not a separate follow-on helper.
Only three local dwords are initialized before the null-camera path copies a fourth untouched local into output+0x0c; the correction accurately records this uninitialized component.
Raw branch timing distinguishes the event-4000 schedule that adds init+0x3d8 from the alternate global-time schedule and snapshot path, exactly matching the proposed branch correction.

The proposed comment falsely says RET 0x8 proves eventPayload plus unusedContext; fresh raw instructions show the sole epilogue is RET 0x4, so the manifest row is rejected even though its out-of-line reader-binding call identities are otherwise useful.
The global current value is loaded only after effect cleanup and is then moved into the saved slot on both paths; it is not a pre-walk pointer as the old comment claimed.
The entire body is FLD [ECX+0x60] followed by RET, with no boolean conversion or completion test; the conservative float-field getter name/comment matches the evidence.
The function dereferences ECX before testing it; that later test selects ECX+8 versus null for an argument and cannot be a receiver-null guard, so the old comment is false.
The entry is a single JMP to CActor__dtor_base and is called from a shared deleting destructor; target-expanded cleanup does not make the entry an inline body.
Raw stores show the unscaled calculation goes to event+0x64 and the distance-scaled calculation to +0x68, the reverse of the inherited account.
The second stack dword is explicitly pushed with sound_event into StopSound and RET 0x8 proves both inputs; the decompiler omission is a stale callee-prototype artifact.
Raw code pushes literal 1 and sound_event into StopSound; RET 0x4 proves the sample input, while backend handling of the block flag remains runtime-unproven.
The raw body reads the sole reset_after_render byte and conditionally saves/clears cursors and toggles the buffer slot; the decompiler's constant-zero flag is wrong.
Stable stack accounting identifies reset_after_render and its nonzero cursor-save/clear path; RET 0x0c and render callers support the three-argument boundary.
Raw code reads reset_after_render and conditionally saves/clears cursors in this direct bind/draw path; the body lacks the FVF, pending-state, and validation work of adjacent variants.
All four trace branches call CWorld__FindFirstThingToHitLine and no OID__TraceLineAndSelectBestTargetHit call exists; the remaining ballistic/attachment/fear-grid account is body-supported.

Prologue stack accounting maps three callee-popped inputs after ECX: mem_buffer, is_base_world, and initialize_world_state; 123 buffer reads, the header call, the flag gate, and RET 0x0c independently establish this structured prototype correction.
The function compares 0x88760868 for D3DERR_DEVICELOST, polls until nonnegative, sleeps, and sets a global byte; there is no reset call, correcting both the constant and behavior claim.
Raw stack accounting shows both success and failure write entry arg3 while arg2 is unread; the body computes vector length into an allocated scalar wrapper and RETs 0x0c.
Both paths use entry arg3 as the result pointer, arg1 as input, and leave arg2 unread; the static-shadow sample and scalar-wrapper construction support the corrected ABI note.
Success and failure both write entry arg3, arg1 supplies the vector, and arg2 is unused; the split decompiler labels are stack-recovery artifacts.
Raw code extracts vector +4 from arg1 and writes the scalar or null through arg3 while arg2 is unread, matching the corrected handler boundary.
Raw code extracts vector +8 from arg1 and writes the scalar or null through arg3 while arg2 is unread, matching the corrected handler boundary.
The handler tests the unit flag, reads the first script argument, and directly calls CUnit__SetFactionForHierarchy(this,int); the corrected comment names the proven callee.
The handler reads slot_index, calls CWorld__GetWorldTextSlotTimerValue, and writes a float wrapper through entry arg3 on success/failure; unrelated stale signature/tag debt remains outside this row.
The complete entry is one JMP to CDXSurf__dtor, structured metadata marks the thunk target, and the deleting-destructor caller corroborates the correction.
The entry is one JMP to CGame__dtor, already has thunk metadata, and is called by CDXGame__scalar_deleting_dtor; the expanded teardown belongs to the target.
Raw COM-call arguments bind stream 0 with offset 0 and stride 0x14 from the documented device buffer; the decompiler hides these arguments but the render-state/draw flow agrees.
Raw state IDs prove stage-0 TEXTURETRANSFORMFLAGS handling, and the overlay-global branch disables RS 0x1b before reading enable_overlay; only stage-1 TEXCOORDINDEX is gated by that byte.
Raw code forwards entry stack arg1 into CWaterRenderSystem__LoadTextures before RET 0x4; the saved fastcall signatures omit that value, a separate artifact outside this comment correction.
The locale callback calls CRT__IsLocaleIdAllowedByLocaleMap and no codepage argument is consulted; on success it sets the country match bit and locale globals.

Raw stack accounting, the TreeIteratorNext call, caller pushes, and RET 0x8 prove two explicit arguments and successor writeback; the stale three-argument signature/tag are separate debt outside this comment row.
The 0x14-byte node stores parent at +0x04 and the 0/1 red-black color at +0x10; the live name is already corrected, so only the stale instructional comment needs replacement.

The wrapper carries one value in EAX and pushes five stack inputs; the callee copies EAX to ESI and RETs 0x14, so ESI is not a sixth independent stack argument and custom-storage debt remains unresolved.
The function is a sole JMP through global slot 0x656f84, and both known callers are Math matrix builders, supporting the bounded Math dispatch-slot name while retaining unknown target semantics.
The fresh body calls the now-resolved DispatchTableCall_656f3c during optional transform passes, so the corrected comment removes the stale unresolved-callee claim.
The body has one explicit file_handle and RET 0x4; the saved extra write_enabled parameter is phantom, so the corrected comment avoids attributing behavior to it.
Pointer setup anchors the palette base at this+0x38, not this+0x40; indexed lookups use the corrected base while the packing role remains unchanged.
Raw PUSH 1 plus two consecutive INC ESI instructions prove a two-byte output stride, contradicting the inherited +4 stride claim.
The diagnostic_format input is compared with the literal syntax-error string; it is not a token-value comparison, so the corrected comment fixes the operand role.
Fresh xrefs are buffer/macro/include initialization callers and do not include HandleDirective_If, so the corrected comment removes that false caller attribution.
The body is the libjpeg compression-context initialization path, not decompression; the corrected name aligns the proven API family without changing metadata beyond the name.
The libjpeg control flow and callers establish finish_compress rather than decompression teardown, supporting the corrected name/comment.
The libjpeg pipeline enters compression startup; the corrected comment withdraws the stale decompression interpretation while retaining bounded static evidence.
The function writes JPEG scanlines in the compression pipeline, so both owner/action and the corrected comment are supported by body/caller structure.
The function sets the JPEG compressor color space; the prior decompression-family identity conflicts with its call graph and library control flow.
The body selects the default compressor color space, not a decompressor setting, matching the corrected name/comment and surrounding libjpeg sequence.
Allocation, field initialization, and decode-job usage support a descriptor object; the corrected comment narrows the previous stronger class/ownership claim.
Raw PFCMPEQ/MOVD/JNZ yields a -1 mask for a zero determinant, not zero, so the corrected comment fixes the branch-result semantics.
ECX supplies the scale and IMUL applies it through kind-7 wrappers during node-tree serialization; the corrected comment fixes the operand ownership.
Raw stack access and RET 0x8 prove two explicit inputs; the corrected output-stage comment restores the omitted second argument without overclaiming its runtime effect.
Raw arguments identify window size and callback despite lossy decompilation; the corrected comment describes the inflate/decode-window allocation boundary at static confidence.
The second stack input is a mode value at the stable frame and RET 0x8 proves both inputs, so the corrected JPEG component-buffer comment fixes the omitted argument.
The allocated controller is preserved in EBX and a second mode input is read before RET 0x8, supporting the corrected working-buffer initialization account.
The 9,817-instruction function has no calls, 3,497 unique literal-string returns, and one RET 0x4, confirming a large HRESULT lookup table rather than a generic helper body.
Raw unwind entry ABI and caller structure establish the corrected callee-pop/continuation wording; the change remains a bounded static unwind annotation.
Raw unwind entry ABI and caller structure establish the corrected callee-pop/continuation wording; the change remains a bounded static unwind annotation.
